commit.message:
feat: MetadataCompiler — dense struct-of-arrays runtime tables (#1059)

Add MetadataCompiler that converts DataEntityMetadata/DataFieldMetadata into
cache-friendly ordinal-indexed runtime tables at startup:

- EntityTable: struct-of-arrays indexed by contiguous EntityId, sorted by slug
  with binary-search slug→EntityId resolver
- FieldTable: struct-of-arrays indexed by contiguous FieldId, entity-grouped
  with packed FieldFlags and WireFieldType mapping
- RouteTable: flat dispatch array indexed by (entityId << 4) | verbOrdinal
  for O(1) handler resolution
- RuntimeSnapshot: immutable container with atomic Interlocked.Exchange swap
- MetadataCompiler.Compile/CompileAndSwap: assigns contiguous IDs, builds all
  tables, returns frozen snapshot
- Wired into startup (after RuntimeEntityRegistry.Build) and gallery rebuild
- 11 unit tests covering empty/single/multi entity compilation, slug lookup,
  flag packing, wire-type mapping, route dispatch, and atomic swap

Closes #1056

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore(core-xml): bump fast-xml-parser minimum to ^5.3.7 to fix critical CVEs (#37489) `@azure/core-xml` declared `fast-xml-parser: ^5.0.7`, allowing versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 affected by two critical CVEs to satisfy the dependency range. ### Changes - **`sdk/core/core-xml/package.json`**: Raise minimum `fast-xml-parser` from `^5.0.7` → `^5.3.7` - **`sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md`**: Document security fix in unreleased `1.5.1` section ### Packages impacted by this PR `@azure/core-xml` ### Issues associated with this PR - [GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj) — DoS via unbounded entity expansion in DOCTYPE - [GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2) — Entity encoding bypass via regex injection in DOCTYPE entity names ### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR `fast-xml-parser` versions 4.1.3–5.3.5 have two critical vulnerabilities. The floating `^5.0.7` range allows any vulnerable 5.x version to be resolved. Raising the floor to `^5.3.7` ensures only patched versions are used. ### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR chosen? Only option: update the minimum version constraint. No API changes required; `fast-xml-parser` 5.3.7 is backwards-compatible. ### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_ No. This is a dependency version floor bump with no behavioral changes — existing tests provide sufficient coverage. ### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_ Prior art: [1.3.4 bump of fast-xml-parser to `^4.2.4`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/blob/main/sdk/core/core-xml/CHANGELOG.md#134-2023-06-20) for a prior security issue. ### Checklists - [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description. - [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so, create an Issue in the [Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript) repository and link it here.)_ - [x] Added a changelog (if necessary).
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > Security vulnerability: fast-xml-parser > It looks like there is a dependency on v5.3.1 of fast-xml-parser: > > > > fast-xml-parser 4.1.3 - 5.3.5 > Severity: critical > fast-xml-parser affected by DoS through entity expansion in DOCTYPE (no expansion limit) - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jmr7-xgp7-cmfj > fast-xml-parser has an entity encoding bypass via regex injection in DOCTYPE entity names - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-m7jm-9gc2-mpf2 > > Upgrade minimum for fast-xml-parser in core-xml to ^5.3.7 > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > @timovv > Hi @skhilliard, since `@azure/core-xml` takes a floating dependency on `fast-xml-parser@^5.0.7`, it will accept any version 5.0.7 or above and below 6.0.0. We can update the minimum here, but in the meantime, you should be able to resolve this issue immediately by doing a dependency upgrade (`npm update`, etc.), which will allow you to pull in a patched version of `fast-xml-parser`. > > Let us know if you have any questions. > >
- Fixes Azure/azure-sdk-for-js#37344 --- 💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions, customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: timovv <1787642+timovv@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: accept Azure DevOps Git URLs without .git suffix in plugin marketplace (#299576)

* Initial plan

* fix: accept Azure DevOps Git URLs without .git suffix in plugin marketplace

- Remove the `.git` suffix requirement from `normalizeGitRepoPath`; the function
  now accepts any URL path that has at least two segments.
- Update `parseUriMarketplaceReference` to handle paths with and without `.git`:
  - `cacheSegments` are built without the suffix in both cases.
  - `canonicalId` is always normalized to include `.git` so that the same repo

Locally this is what I see: - Root cause: `@fluidframework/react/alpha` re-exports quill-dependent text editor components. Quill's `emitter.js` calls `document.addEventListener()` at the **module's top level**, before any test `before()` hook can set up jsdom. - Fix: add `src/test/mochaHooks.ts` that calls `globalJsdom()` at module level, loaded via Node's `--import` flag (`.mocharc.cjs`) so it runs before mocha evaluates any test files or modules imported (in this case, transitively) by them. This is the same pattern already used by the `text-editor` example package. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix inline PR review comments not being fetched (#294)

* Fix inline PR review comments not being fetched

`gh pr view --json reviews` does not include inline code review comments
(comments attached to specific diff lines). This meant erg only forwarded
the top-level review body to Claude, silently dropping the actual inline
suggestions (e.g. from Copilot or human reviewers).

to retrieve inline review comments. The REST API failure is non-fatal so
review bodies are still forwarded if the inline fetch fails.

fix: make topic/temperature/stopword changes permanent

The previous fixes to quality_config.json were overwritten by
quality_guardian.py which regenerates that file every run. Move
fixes to their permanent homes:

- content.json topic_seeds: replace 50 platform-meta seeds with 50
  diverse real-world topics (dumplings, bridges, QWERTY, etc.)
- content.json stop_words: add 28 common words (food, time, city,
  state, code, etc.) so they never get banned as 'overused'
- quality_guardian.py: set base temperature_adjustment to 0.1
  (was 0.0 unless diversity dropped below 0.7)

commit.message:
feat(llm): configurable timeouts via env vars, bump defaults

New env vars (all optional, backward-compatible):
  LLM_TIMEOUT_HTTP=600      # httpx total timeout per request (was 300s hardcoded)
  LLM_TIMEOUT_CONNECT=30    # httpx connect timeout (unchanged)
  LLM_TIMEOUT_MLX_WAIT=120  # seconds to wait for local-mlx server ready (was 60s)

Changes:
- Add _LLM_TIMEOUT_HTTP / _LLM_TIMEOUT_CONNECT / _LLM_TIMEOUT_MLX_WAIT module constants
- _get_http(): AsyncClient(timeout=_LLM_TIMEOUT_HTTP)
- stream client: Timeout(connect=..., read=_LLM_TIMEOUT_HTTP, ...)
- _ensure_mlx_server(): loop over _LLM_TIMEOUT_MLX_WAIT iterations (was hardcoded 60)
- Warning message now shows actual wait value

Rationale: skill evals with local-mlx were timing out at 300s on large models;
local-mlx startup was failing silently after 60s on cold start.
Add to .env: LLM_TIMEOUT_HTTP=600, LLM_TIMEOUT_MLX_WAIT=120

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(executor): nudge MiniMax to call tools when round-0 returns short text

When the LLM has tools available but returns < 250 chars of prose on round 0
(typical MiniMax 'Je comprends, je vais explorer...' behavior), inject a mandatory
tool-call reminder and retry the round instead of accepting the empty response.

Fixes AC improvement cycle agents (ac-architect, ac-codex) writing text introductions
instead of calling code_write on their first turn.

feat: context blocks steps 12-14 — RecapAgent arc blocks, session memory writes

Step 12: RecapAgent now accepts arc_blocks from ContextBlockStore; uses them
  as primary context over raw arc_history for multi-arc recaps. Turn pipeline
  fetches arc + quest blocks before recap task creation.

Step 13: KA voice journal — SessionMemoryWriter writes end-of-session style
  annotation to bible.planning_data['voice_journal']; loaded into KeyAnimator
  at session startup via _build_profile_dna voice calibration section.

Step 14: Director session memo — SessionMemoryWriter writes structured arc/seed/
  NPC memo to planning_data['director_session_memo']; included in next Director
  review via existing planning_data dump.

Wiring: Orchestrator.async_close() fires both writes, awaits with gather, saves
  to bible. Lifespan handler calls async_close() before reset_orchestrator().
  get_orchestrator_optional() added for non-creating access.
